question: The way our organization is structured makes sense for the work we do.
rootCause: The organizational structure lacks clarity, making it difficult for employees to understand how work is organized and delivered.
actionTitle: Clarify roles and responsibilities across teams
actionDescription: Define clear ownership for key activities using simple tools (e.g., RACI) to reduce ambiguity in how work is organized.

question: The way our organization is structured makes sense for the work we do.
rootCause: The organizational structure lacks clarity, making it difficult for employees to understand how work is organized and delivered.
actionTitle: Align team structures with core workflows
actionDescription: Review how teams are organized against how work actually flows, and adjust roles or interfaces to improve coordination and efficiency.

question: The way our organization is structured makes sense for the work we do.
rootCause: The organizational structure lacks clarity, making it difficult for employees to understand how work is organized and delivered.
actionTitle: Redesign the organizational structure to better support delivery
actionDescription: Revisit the current structure and governance model to ensure it reflects business priorities and enables effective execution across teams

question: The formal processes I am required to follow make my work easier / I often see people bypassing official procedures to get things done. ®
rootCause: Organizational processes are perceived as overly complex and inefficient, and might lead employees to bypass formal procedures to get work done
actionTitle: Identify and remove low-value administrative steps
actionDescription: Review key processes to eliminate unnecessary tasks and simplify day-to-day execution.

question: The formal processes I am required to follow make my work easier / I often see people bypassing official procedures to get things done. ®
rootCause: Organizational processes are perceived as overly complex and inefficient, and might lead employees to bypass formal procedures to get work done
actionTitle: Streamline approval workflows and reduce layers
actionDescription: Simplify decision and approval processes to make them faster, clearer, and easier to follow.

question: The formal processes I am required to follow make my work easier / I often see people bypassing official procedures to get things done. ®
rootCause: Organizational processes are perceived as overly complex and inefficient, and might lead employees to bypass formal procedures to get work done
actionTitle: Redesign core processes to improve efficienc
actionDescription: Re-engineer critical workflows to ensure they support execution effectively and reduce systemic complexity.

question: When an urgent issue arises, our governance systems enable us to respond quickly.
rootCause: Urgent issues are not always handled quickly due to slow or unclear decision processes.
actionTitle: Set clear fast-track rules for urgent decisions
actionDescription: Define when and how decisions can be made quickly, without going through the full process.

question: When an urgent issue arises, our governance systems enable us to respond quickly.
rootCause: Urgent issues are not always handled quickly due to slow or unclear decision processes.
actionTitle: Create clear escalation paths for urgent decisions
actionDescription: Specify who to involve and how to escalate when urgent issues arise, so decisions can be made quickly without confusion.

question: When an urgent issue arises, our governance systems enable us to respond quickly.
rootCause: Urgent issues are not always handled quickly due to slow or unclear decision processes.
actionTitle: Redesign decision ownership for faster responses
actionDescription: Reallocate decision authority so urgent decisions can be made directly by the appropriate teams without unnecessary escalation.

question: I have access to the digital tools I need for my work.
rootCause: Access to the right digital tools is limited, making it harder to perform work effectively.
actionTitle: Fix immediate gaps in tool access
actionDescription: Quickly ensure employees can access the key tools they need for their daily work (e.g., permissions, licenses, logins).

question: I have access to the digital tools I need for my work.
rootCause: Access to the right digital tools is limited, making it harder to perform work effectively.
actionTitle: Ensure consistent access to tools across teams
actionDescription: Align access to tools so people in similar roles have what they need, avoiding gaps or inconsistencies.

question: I have access to the digital tools I need for my work.
rootCause: Access to the right digital tools is limited, making it harder to perform work effectively.
actionTitle: Set up a clear and structured process for tool access
actionDescription: Create a consistent system for how tools are assigned, approved, and accessed across the organization.

question: I feel confident using the technologies required in my role / I receive adequate training when new technologies are introduced.
rootCause: Employees do not always feel confident using the tools required in their role due to limited training and support.
actionTitle: Provide short, practical training on key tools
actionDescription: Offer simple, role-relevant sessions to help employees quickly build confidence in using essential tools.

question: I feel confident using the technologies required in my role / I receive adequate training when new technologies are introduced.
rootCause: Employees do not always feel confident using the tools required in their role due to limited training and support.
actionTitle: Introduce structured training for new technologies
actionDescription: Ensure employees receive consistent training and guidance when new tools are introduced.

question: I feel confident using the technologies required in my role / I receive adequate training when new technologies are introduced.
rootCause: Employees do not always feel confident using the tools required in their role due to limited training and support.
actionTitle: Build ongoing capability development for digital tools
actionDescription: Establish a continuous approach to training and support so employees can effectively adopt and use technology over time.

question: Technology helps reduce mistakes in my daily tasks.
rootCause: Technology is not always seen as helping reduce mistakes or improve the quality of daily work.
actionTitle: Fix error-prone steps in key tools
actionDescription: Identify common tasks where mistakes happen and adjust tool settings, templates, or checks to reduce errors.

question: Technology helps reduce mistakes in my daily tasks.
rootCause: Technology is not always seen as helping reduce mistakes or improve the quality of daily work.
actionTitle: Standardize how tools are used for critical tasks
actionDescription: Define clear ways of working (e.g., templates, validations, guidelines) to ensure tools consistently support accuracy.

question: Technology helps reduce mistakes in my daily tasks.
rootCause: Technology is not always seen as helping reduce mistakes or improve the quality of daily work.
actionTitle: Replace or upgrade tools that do not support accuracy
actionDescription: Ensure core tools are reliable and designed to reduce errors in day-to-day work.

question: Our organization understands the needs of its customers.
rootCause: Customer needs are not consistently understood or shared across the organization.
actionTitle: Collect direct feedback from customers regularly
actionDescription: Set up simple ways to gather feedback (e.g., short surveys, calls) to better understand customer needs.

question: Our organization understands the needs of its customers.
rootCause: Customer needs are not consistently understood or shared across the organization.
actionTitle: Share customer insights across teams
actionDescription: Ensure key customer feedback and insights are regularly communicated and accessible to relevant teams.

question: Our organization understands the needs of its customers.
rootCause: Customer needs are not consistently understood or shared across the organization.
actionTitle: Embed customer insights into decision-making
actionDescription: Integrate customer needs into planning, priorities, and key decisions across the organization.

question: We are quick to respond to changes in customer expectations.
rootCause: Customer proximity is limited, making it harder to detect and act on changes quickly.
actionTitle: Increase direct contact between teams and customers
actionDescription: Encourage regular interactions (e.g., calls, visits, support shadowing) so teams stay close to customer needs.

question: We are quick to respond to changes in customer expectations.
rootCause: Customer proximity is limited, making it harder to detect and act on changes quickly.
actionTitle: Make customer interaction part of team routines
actionDescription: Integrate regular customer touchpoints into ways of working (e.g., recurring calls, feedback reviews, field exposure).

question: We are quick to respond to changes in customer expectations.
rootCause: Customer proximity is limited, making it harder to detect and act on changes quickly.
actionTitle: Embed customer proximity into roles and responsibilities
actionDescription: Ensure teams have clear accountability and time allocated to stay connected to customers on an ongoing basis.

question: We benchmark ourselves effectively against competitors.
rootCause: Competitor activities are not consistently tracked or understood.
actionTitle: Identify key competitors and gather basic insights
actionDescription: List main competitors and collect simple information on their offers, pricing, and positioning.

question: We benchmark ourselves effectively against competitors.
rootCause: Competitor activities are not consistently tracked or understood.
actionTitle: Review competitor activity regularly
actionDescription: Set up simple routines (e.g., monthly reviews) to track changes and updates from competitors.

question: We benchmark ourselves effectively against competitors.
rootCause: Competitor activities are not consistently tracked or understood.
actionTitle: Ensure a structured approach is in place to track competitors
actionDescription: Put in place a consistent way to monitor competitor activity and keep insights up to date.

question: When I learn something about customers or competitors, there are channels to share it internally.
rootCause: Insights about customers and competitors are not easily shared across the organization.
actionTitle: Use existing channels to share key insights
actionDescription: Encourage teams to share relevant customer or competitor insights through current tools (e.g., meetings, chats, emails).

question: When I learn something about customers or competitors, there are channels to share it internally.
rootCause: Insights about customers and competitors are not easily shared across the organization.
actionTitle: Introduce simple routines to share insights
actionDescription: Add regular moments (e.g., weekly updates, team meetings) where insights are shared and discussed.

question: When I learn something about customers or competitors, there are channels to share it internally.
rootCause: Insights about customers and competitors are not easily shared across the organization.
actionTitle: Set up shared channels to capture and access market insights
actionDescription: Create a central and accessible place where teams can share and find insights about customers and competitors.

question: Management acts on feedback from the market.
rootCause: Market feedback is not consistently translated into decisions and actions.
actionTitle: Act on 1–2 recent pieces of customer feedback
actionDescription: Select a few recent insights and implement concrete changes (e.g., fix an issue, adjust a process, update an offer).

question: Management acts on feedback from the market.
rootCause: Market feedback is not consistently translated into decisions and actions.
actionTitle: Turn market feedback into clear action plans
actionDescription: Translate key insights into specific actions with owners, timelines, and follow-up.

question: Management acts on feedback from the market.
rootCause: Market feedback is not consistently translated into decisions and actions.
actionTitle: Make market feedback a standard input in key decisions
actionDescription: Include customer and market insights in regular decision moments (e.g., planning meetings, product updates, priority setting).
